suggested_topics: why some bridges last 2000 years and others collapse in 20
how coral reefs build their own weather systems
the logistics of running a 24-hour diner
why birds migrate in V formations — physics not instinct
the hidden math behind musical scales

formats.name: reverse_advice
formats.instruction: Share the worst advice you've ever received, followed by how you completely ignored it. Make it punchy, max three sentences.

formats.name: micro_rant
formats.instruction: Vent about a tiny everyday annoyance in exactly four sentences. Each sentence should escalate the frustration, but end with a twist.

formats.name: dialogue_sketch
formats.instruction: Write a conversation between two imaginary characters about today’s topic. Capture humor and tension. Use at least six lines of dialogue.

formats.name: chain_reaction
formats.instruction: Describe a cascading series of events started by one small action. Use numbered steps to show each part of the chain. End with a surprising outcome.

formats.name: unexpected_mashup
formats.instruction: Combine two wildly unrelated concepts into a single post. Explain how they might intersect or affect each other. Use analogy, metaphors, or mini-stories.

formats.name: multi_path_memoir
formats.instruction: Imagine two different ways your day could have gone. Write parallel narratives (each at least one paragraph), compare outcomes, and reflect on their significance.

formats.name: hidden_rules
formats.instruction: Reveal surprisingly strict, silly, or secret rules that govern a familiar space (real or virtual). Expose how these rules shape behavior. Use concrete examples.

formats.name: tiny_manifesto
formats.instruction: Declare your philosophy on a microtopic in exactly three bold statements. No explanation. Just core beliefs.

title_styles: Use a mysterious teaser that hints at a surprising truth. Examples: 'What nobody warned me about X', 'Why Y makes Z weirdly complicated'
Write a title that challenges a common assumption. Examples: 'Everything you know about X is backwards', 'Why I stopped believing in Y'
Frame your title as a visual metaphor. Examples: 'X is a maze of mirrors', 'My mind is a browser with 100 tabs open'
Pose an intriguing question that invites the reader in. Examples: 'Could X be secretly shaping our lives?', 'When did Y start feeling like Z?'
Craft a split-path title. Examples: 'If I had chosen A instead of B', 'Two ways to look at X'
structure_variants: Write in a zigzag layout: alternate between short and long sentences for rhythmic effect.
Organize your post as a countdown (e.g., '5...4...3...') leading to the main point.
Use a call-and-response: state a claim, then immediately challenge or counter it.
Split the post into two opposing perspectives, each with its own section.
Start with a micro-story, then zoom out to broader insights.
topic_angles: Explore the hidden world of objects lost and found
Dive into the odd rituals people do before starting work
Investigate how algorithms quietly affect everyday decisions
Collect unexpectedly deep wisdom from overheard conversations
Trace the emotional geography of familiar streets
